referenced_message.author: ferros428
referenced_message.content:
Honestly, there are many things that need to be improved in the DZ

1- the time I limit is way too short. It needs to be either removed (preferable) or greatly increased

2- There needs to be way more Heroic landmarks, even if this means adding a seperate high level DZ for endgame grinding

3- relative to the size of the map, there needs to be more landmarks. I feel like I’m spending more time getting to the landmarks than actually clearing them

4- Slightly increasing the default sprinting speed

Expanding on point 1. If you guys REALLY want there to be time limit, a good compromise would be so that when the time limit does expire, your mask slowly looses durability like in high contamination areas. 

This would give players the option to stay in the DZ but at a cost

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
Personally, I would prefer the Dark Zone to have no time limit at all.

The Dark Zone should feel like a persistent, open PvP environment rather than an activity that resets after a short session. Allowing players to remain in the DZ as long as they choose would create a more organic experience and encourage long-term player interactions.

However, removing the timer would require balancing mechanisms to prevent abuse.

One solution would be to have contamination slowly accumulate on a player's mask simply by being present in the Dark Zone. Players would eventually need to return to a safe house to clear contamination before continuing. This would naturally create movement around the map and prevent players from remaining in a single location indefinitely.

Heroic landmarks should also generate significantly more contamination than normal DZ activity. This would discourage groups from repeatedly farming the same Heroic landmark for hours without interruption.

I would also like to see the Hunt system return.

A revised Hunt system could provide increasing rewards for players who take risks:

Higher Hunt levels grant increased luck or drop-rate bonuses.

Rogue players who continue eliminating agents eventually become Manhunt targets.

Manhunt targets are permanently marked on the map for all players.

Manhunt players receive increased rewards and luck bonuses.

Non-rogue players receive meaningful rewards for successfully hunting and eliminating Manhunt targets.

This creates a risk-versus-reward system that encourages PvP while giving both Rogues and SHD agents incentives to participate.

If the team decides to keep the 20-minute structure, I would prefer seeing it moved into a separate Dark Zone experience rather than being the only DZ option.

For example, a dedicated survival-style mode similar to Survival from the original Division could be introduced:

Players enter with only basic green gear and a starter pistol.

Equipment is earned through exploration, combat, and looting.

Players must successfully extract to keep their rewards.

Extraction could culminate in a fight against a Rogue Agent, similar to the Lone Wolf encounter.

Any mode-specific equipment should remain exclusive to that mode to avoid balance issues in the main game.

One of my biggest disappointments in TDR was not seeing a Survival-inspired mode return. Survival was one of the most unique and memorable experiences in the original Division, and I believe a modern version could become one of the most popular activities in the game.

Overall, my preference would be a permanent, open Dark Zone with systems that encourage movement, risk, and player interaction rather than a fixed session timer.

referenced_message.author: riggygaming
referenced_message.content: Can we keep this topic relevant to the overarching topic of the DZ Time Limit thank you!

content: Can we keep this topic relevant to the overarching topic of the DZ Time Limit thank you!

The current top time on the NA server is actually a 2 DPS, 2 support composition: 1 Aegis, 1 Recon, and 2 Commandos.
Personally, I think every class has at least one or two issues that could be improved, not just a couple of specific specs. The only support class I consistently see being passed over for Elite content is Medic, and that's largely because its role is almost entirely focused on healing. It doesn't bring much additional utility or damage to the team. That said, Medic is extremely strong in PvP right now.
A very common speed-run composition is still 1 tank and 3 DPS, which shows that support and tank roles can absolutely have a place in endgame content.
As for the grind, I completely understand that it takes time. With the loot system being heavily RNG-based, what you get is largely up to chance. When I was farming gear for my endgame Commando build, I ended up collecting around 90% of the gear needed for five other specs along the way. In a sense, I was progressing multiple classes at once.
My recommendation is not to immediately discard gear just because it isn't for the spec you're currently playing. Check every piece and consider whether it could be useful for another build. Just last night, while farming upgrades for my Commando, I found three excellent pieces for other classes without ever having to switch off my main character.

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
I understand that, but as a whole it can be abused and is currently being abused. 
Thats my core issue. The Devs add these things without thinking of how sweats will manipulate those mechanics for an edge. 
There are other classes you can pick to solo. So I dont think supports should get that damage buff. They are designed to play with people. Because it exists people can abuse it to do a ridiculous amount of damage in one shot. 
I still feel a support, solo or not shouldnt have this. A support is exactly what it is. Someone who supports a team, not someone who can abuse game mechanics to get an edge. They shouldn't get a buff because they want to play solo. This game is designed for everyone to be able to play all ten specs anytime they want. So removing the damage buff from support would stop the abusers from having a edge and force players to play the game as designed.

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
Personally, I would prefer the Dark Zone to have no time limit at all.

The Dark Zone should feel like a persistent, open PvP environment rather than an activity that resets after a short session. Allowing players to remain in the DZ as long as they choose would create a more organic experience and encourage long-term player interactions.

However, removing the timer would require balancing mechanisms to prevent abuse.

One solution would be to have contamination slowly accumulate on a player's mask simply by being present in the Dark Zone. Players would eventually need to return to a safe house to clear contamination before continuing. This would naturally create movement around the map and prevent players from remaining in a single location indefinitely.

Heroic landmarks should also generate significantly more contamination than normal DZ activity. This would discourage groups from repeatedly farming the same Heroic landmark for hours without interruption.

I would also like to see the Hunt system return.

A revised Hunt system could provide increasing rewards for players who take risks:

Higher Hunt levels grant increased luck or drop-rate bonuses.

Rogue players who continue eliminating agents eventually become Manhunt targets.

Manhunt targets are permanently marked on the map for all players.

Manhunt players receive increased rewards and luck bonuses.

Non-rogue players receive meaningful rewards for successfully hunting and eliminating Manhunt targets.

This creates a risk-versus-reward system that encourages PvP while giving both Rogues and SHD agents incentives to participate.

If the team decides to keep the 20-minute structure, I would prefer seeing it moved into a separate Dark Zone experience rather than being the only DZ option.

For example, a dedicated survival-style mode similar to Survival from the original Division could be introduced:

Players enter with only basic green gear and a starter pistol.

Equipment is earned through exploration, combat, and looting.

Players must successfully extract to keep their rewards.

Extraction could culminate in a fight against a Rogue Agent, similar to the Lone Wolf encounter.

Any mode-specific equipment should remain exclusive to that mode to avoid balance issues in the main game.

One of my biggest disappointments in TDR was not seeing a Survival-inspired mode return. Survival was one of the most unique and memorable experiences in the original Division, and I believe a modern version could become one of the most popular activities in the game.

Overall, my preference would be a permanent, open Dark Zone with systems that encourage movement, risk, and player interaction rather than a fixed session timer.

Personally, I would prefer the Dark Zone to have no time limit at all.

The Dark Zone should feel like a persistent, open PvP environment rather than an activity that resets after a short session. Allowing players to remain in the DZ as long as they choose would create a more organic experience and encourage long-term player interactions.

However, removing the timer would require balancing mechanisms to prevent abuse.

One solution would be to have contamination slowly accumulate on a player's mask simply by being present in the Dark Zone. Players would eventually need to return to a safe house to clear contamination before continuing. This would naturally create movement around the map and prevent players from remaining in a single location indefinitely.

Heroic landmarks should also generate significantly more contamination than normal DZ activity. This would discourage groups from repeatedly farming the same Heroic landmark for hours without interruption.

I would also like to see the Hunt system return.

A revised Hunt system could provide increasing rewards for players who take risks:

Higher Hunt levels grant increased luck or drop-rate bonuses.

Rogue players who continue eliminating agents eventually become Manhunt targets.

Manhunt targets are permanently marked on the map for all players.

Manhunt players receive increased rewards and luck bonuses.

Non-rogue players receive meaningful rewards for successfully hunting and eliminating Manhunt targets.

This creates a risk-versus-reward system that encourages PvP while giving both Rogues and SHD agents incentives to participate.

If the team decides to keep the 20-minute structure, I would prefer seeing it moved into a separate Dark Zone experience rather than being the only DZ option.

For example, a dedicated survival-style mode similar to Survival from the original Division could be introduced:

Players enter with only basic green gear and a starter pistol.

Equipment is earned through exploration, combat, and looting.

Players must successfully extract to keep their rewards.

Extraction could culminate in a fight against a Rogue Agent, similar to the Lone Wolf encounter.

Any mode-specific equipment should remain exclusive to that mode to avoid balance issues in the main game.

One of my biggest disappointments in TDR was not seeing a Survival-inspired mode return. Survival was one of the most unique and memorable experiences in the original Division, and I believe a modern version could become one of the most popular activities in the game.

Overall, my preference would be a permanent, open Dark Zone with systems that encourage movement, risk, and player interaction rather than a fixed session timer.
Please remove the time completely.

Timer to be removed 
 pick up items from stashes to extend time
 Keeping with current  mechanics add when certain objectives are done. “Extract items via helicopter” then extend time

thats why using the mask as the way to force players to not camp would be important, you can basically have it so that the mask even outside of contam areas only lasts 20mins before it breaks so therefore requiring to be fixed before 20mins and the additional and existing zones which increase that rate. have the repair stations at the edges of the map for example where the exits are also causes everyone to have to run there and get it fixed before their mask breaks.

If you want it to go further in depth you could have 2 durability bars - 1 for the contamination which is already implemented which can you can replace the "filters" in safe houses.. and 1 for the durability of the mask which requires players to run to the exists to "repair" their mask before it runs out, this makes it so that you arnt facing people who just stand in heroic boss zones forever but also dont have to go through 2 loading screens, matchmaking and etc just to get back into dz

Pushing the idea even further you can have "contamination storms" which rolls through 1-2 times a day where that contamination effect is increased massively but the rewards are higher or alternatively just have it so while the "storms" are active players cant enter dz. this gives players ample time to not only farm for either better gear or to reset themselves after hours of grinding when the storm rolls by.

The storm can be done by having the 20min timer reappear but have it so that it signals how much time is left until the storm comes...
